content: We can reframe scalable shared memory for multi-agent active learning as a system that naturally leverages hierarchical virtual context management: each tier of the context hierarchy acts as a segmented shared memory bank, where only contextually relevant memory segments are exposed to active learning agents at a given abstraction level, reducing redundant querying and scaling memory access with the number of agents. This structure lets agents pull shared prior observations from coarse global context tiers when exploring new domains, and drill down to fine-grained local context tiers when refining high-uncertainty predictions, cutting synchronization overhead while improving active learning sample efficiency.
source_topics: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
trigger_topic: Scalable shared memory architecture for multi-agent active learning
